commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The 'Review & Confirm' step shows static text labels, but some minor alignment tweaks could improve the data table readability.
issues.fix: Use a grid layout or specific column widths for the labels vs values to ensure consistent vertical alignment.

evaluations.critique: This is a highly polished and functional component that adheres strictly to the design system and logic requirements. The code is modular, uses props effectively, and provides a smooth, state-managed experience for the user. A few small enhancements to ARIA roles in the custom avatar picker would make it fully production-ready.

issues.description: The main 'Onboarding' heading is disproportionately large compared to the rest of the form elements.
issues.fix: Reduce the font size of the main heading to better balance with the form section title.

issues.description: The avatar selection is just a row of emojis without any selection state, borders, or clear interaction design.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in button-like containers with hover effects and a clear 'selected' border or background color.

issues.description: The 'Back' button has extremely low contrast, making it difficult to read even if disabled.
issues.fix: Increase the contrast of the disabled state text or use a standard ghost button style.

issues.description: The spacing between the avatar selection and the navigation buttons is too tight.
issues.fix: Increase the margin-top on the button container to provide more whitespace.

evaluations.critique: The component functionally implements the first step of the request, including the progress bar and required fields. However, it lacks professional polish; the typography is unbalanced, and the avatar selection looks like plain text rather than an interactive UI element. It feels like a functional prototype rather than a finished production-ready interface.

issues.description: The avatar selection uses custom Box/Clickable components without explicit role='radio' or aria-label attributes for screen readers.
issues.fix: Add role='radio', aria-checked, and aria-label attributes to the avatar selection items.

issues.description: Lack of visual feedback (loading state) on the 'Complete Setup' button after submission.
issues.fix: Add an optional loading prop to the submit button that triggers on submission to prevent double-clicks.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows the design system tokens effectively. The step-by-step logic and validation are implemented correctly. Adding slight polish to accessibility (ARIA roles) and button states would elevate this to production-perfect.

issues.description: Avatar selection options are tiny and lack visual framing, making them look like plain text rather than clickable elements.
issues.fix: Increase emoji size and wrap them in styled radio-button tiles with clear hover and selection states.

issues.description: Vertical spacing is tight between the progress bar, headers, and inputs, leading to a cramped appearance.
issues.fix: Add more vertical padding between the step indicator, section title, and form fields.

issues.description: The overall UI looks like a basic wireframe rather than a finished product, lacking depth, padding, and professional styling.
issues.fix: Place the wizard in a centered card component with a subtle shadow and use a more refined design system for buttons and inputs.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully includes all required fields for the first step and shows a functional progress indicator. However, the visual execution is very basic and lacks professional polish, particularly regarding the avatar selection and general spacing.

issues.description: The layout is slightly tight; while standard gaps are used, the review summary block could benefit from more internal padding or a more structured grid for better readability.
issues.fix: Add more padding to the summary Card and consider using a Grid layout or a Table component for the summary pairs instead of repeated horizontal Stacks.

issues.description: The avatar selection uses Card components as buttons without explicit role='button' or keyboard handlers beyond the Clickable wrapper.
issues.fix: Ensure the avatar selection elements are wrapped in a list and have proper aria-labels and keyboard event listeners for accessibility.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional, follows the design contract perfectly, and implements all requested logic cleanly. The use of state and validation is robust, and the UI is professional and well-structured.

issues.description: Avatar selection is just a row of emojis with no selection indicators, hover states, or button framing.
issues.fix: Wrap each emoji in a button/radio container with a border or background that changes when selected or hovered.

issues.description: The 'Next' button appears enabled (blue) even though 'Name' and 'Email' are empty, violating the requirement that Next is disabled until required fields are valid.
issues.fix: Apply a disabled style (e.g., opacity and gray color) and prevent pointer events if the inputs are empty/invalid.

issues.description: The component is pinned to the top-left corner of the viewport with no padding/margin container.
issues.fix: Center the onboarding card in the viewport or add standard container padding.

issues.description: The 'Next' button color is very pale and low contrast, making it look washed out or like a secondary action.
issues.fix: Use a stronger brand color for primary actions to improve contrast and hierarchy.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the requested structure for Step 1, including the progress bar and required fields. However, it lacks the professional polish expected of a modern UI; the avatar selection is particularly weak as it lacks any interactive visual cues, and the validation logic for the Next button appears to be missing or visually unclear.

issues.description: The clickable avatar selection boxes are implemented as generic boxes using Clickable; they lack proper ARIA roles (like radio/button) and keyboard interaction support beyond native Clickable defaults.
issues.fix: Ensure the avatar selection elements use a role='radio' or role='button' and include aria-checked attributes to improve screen reader accessibility.

issues.description: The layout of the summary in Step 3 is functional but lacks a visual separator or grid structure to differentiate the review labels from the values clearly.
issues.fix: Use a standard Table primitive or ensure consistent width spacing for the key-value pairs to improve readability.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly polished and follows all requirements accurately, including complex state management and validation. The design is clean, uses design system tokens effectively, and provides a smooth, intuitive onboarding experience.

issues.description: The 'Next' button appears enabled (blue) even though required fields 'Name' and 'Email' are empty, violating the validation requirement.
issues.fix: Implement a state check that keeps the Next button in a disabled styling/state until both inputs are non-empty and the email is valid.

issues.description: Avatar selection lacks any visual indicator for the selected state or interactive affordance.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in button or radio-style containers with hover effects and a clear border or background highlight for the active selection.

issues.description: Vertical spacing is too tight, especially between labels and inputs, and the component lacks a central container.
issues.fix: Increase margin-bottom on labels and inputs, and wrap the wizard in a centered card component with padding.

issues.description: The UI looks like an unstyled wireframe; the progress bar is a thin line that feels disconnected from the step title.
issues.fix: Use a thicker progress bar with rounded ends and add more white space between the title and the form content.

evaluations.critique: The component technically displays the fields requested for the first step, but it lacks the polish expected of a professional application. The missing selection state for avatars and the non-functional validation on the 'Next' button are significant usability issues.

issues.description: The 'Next' button does not have a visual 'disabled' style indicating it is inactive, though the logic is present.
issues.fix: Ensure the Button component inherits the disabled state styling correctly or wrap it in a logic that handles visual feedback.

issues.description: The avatar selection uses a custom clickable Box rather than a semantic radio group or accessible list, which may not be fully screen-reader friendly.

issues.description: The step transition lacks a visual animation effect; content just swaps instantly.

commit.prompt:

- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Code is truncated mid-Category field in detail panel. The detail panel implementation is incomplete.
issues.fix: Complete the Category field display and close all open Stack/Card tags. Verify all detail fields render properly.

issues.description: Grid filtering logic is broken: when search filters elements, the grid still shows empty spaces for non-matching elements (opacity: 0.3), violating the filtering requirement. Elements should disappear from grid, not fade.
issues.fix: Wrap filtered elements in a separate container or conditionally render only matching elements. Remove the opacity-based fade logic.

issues.description: Periodic table grid layout doesn't account for filtered state visually. When searching, the grid shows ghost cells (faded elements) which creates visual clutter and breaks the expected grid alignment.
issues.fix: Only render filtered elements in the grid; don't render non-matching elements at all. Update grid positioning logic to handle sparse layouts properly.

issues.description: Element cells have 80px minHeight which may be too tall for a compact periodic table design. Hierarchy between heading and caption text could be clearer.
issues.fix: Reduce minHeight to 70px, increase symbol font size, or adjust spacing to improve visual balance.

issues.description: Element cells use keyboard tabIndex={0} but lack keyboard event handlers. Arrow keys for navigation and Enter to select are not implemented.
issues.fix: Add onKeyDown handler to element cells to support Enter and arrow key navigation. Consider using a data grid pattern for keyboard accessibility.

issues.description: No hover state visual feedback on element cells. The transition declaration exists but no hover style (transform, shadow, scale) is applied.
issues.fix: Add CSS hover styles: transform: scale(1.05), boxShadow enhancement, or background lightening on hover.

issues.description: Search field uses SearchField component but no debouncing. Rapid typing may cause performance issues with large element lists.
issues.fix: Wrap handleSearch in a useMemo debounce function (200-300ms delay) to optimize filter recalculation.

issues.description: Element cells use role='button' with tabIndex={0} but are wrapped in Box (not semantic button). Screen readers may not announce correctly. aria-label is present but lacks context about grid position.
issues.fix: Use actual <button> elements or ensure proper ARIA role and state attributes. Consider aria-selected for selected elements.

issues.description: Legend doesn't have semantic structure. Color swatches are purely decorative divs without aria-hidden or role clarification.
issues.fix: Wrap legend in <ul><li> structure, add aria-hidden='true' to decorative boxes, ensure text color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1).

issues.description: Detail panel animation 'fadeIn' is CSS-in-JS inline but not defined. Will fail at runtime.
issues.fix: Define @keyframes fadeIn in a CSS module or remove animation and use immediate render.

issues.description: categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap use hardcoded strings as keys ('nonmetal', 'noble-gas', etc.) but props.elements may have different category values. No fallback handling.
issues.fix: Dynamically generate color maps from unique categories, or add a default case that returns a neutral color for unmapped categories.

issues.description: No validation of element.row and element.col. Invalid grid positions will cause layout breaks.
issues.fix: Add optional chaining and fallback defaults: gridColumn={element.col || 'auto'}, gridRow={element.row || 'auto'}.

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates solid foundational structure with proper use of design tokens, memoization, and callback optimization. However, it has three critical issues: (1) truncated code in the detail panel, (2) broken filter logic that shows faded non-matching elements instead of removing them, and (3) missing keyboard and hover interactions. Accessibility is incomplete with non-semantic button elements and missing keyboard navigation. The grid layout is well-structured but needs fixes for filtered state rendering and visual hover feedback to be production-ready.

issues.description: The legend lists the categories but does not show the associated colors as requested.
issues.fix: Add color swatches or background colors to the legend items to match the elements.

issues.description: The detail panel is not visible below the grid, and there is no placeholder or 'Select an element' message.
issues.fix: Add a dedicated section below the grid for element details with a fallback state.

issues.description: The legend items are just plain text strings with minimal spacing, making them hard to read.
issues.fix: Style legend items as tags or pills with proper spacing and visual grouping.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table grid layout is technically excellent and correctly places elements according to their atomic properties. However, the component fails on the legend requirement by omitting the colors, and the lack of a visible detail panel makes the 'interactive' part of the request hard to verify.

issues.description: Detail panel section is cut off mid-render — the Category label and content are incomplete. The entire detail panel layout appears truncated.
issues.fix: Complete the detail panel Card content with proper closing tags and full Category display section.

issues.description: When search filters elements, non-matching elements remain visually present in grid (opacity 0.3) but still occupy grid cells. This breaks the grid layout when elements are hidden.
issues.fix: Either conditionally render only filteredElements in the grid, or restructure to show all elements but disable/gray out non-matches without layout shift.

issues.description: Legend color swatches are very small (12×12px) and may be hard to distinguish. Category label text could be cut off on smaller screens.
issues.fix: Increase swatch size to 16×16px or 20×20px; use flex-wrap properly to handle legend on mobile.

issues.description: Grid cells have hover state defined only via CSS transition but no explicit hover background or shadow change. Visual feedback is subtle.
issues.fix: Add explicit hover style (e.g., `&:hover { transform: scale(1.05); box-shadow: ... }`) to make element selection more obvious.

issues.description: Search field clears selection state implicitly but there's no explicit feedback when typing (no 'no results' message if search yields 0 elements).
issues.fix: Add conditional alert/empty state when filteredElements.length === 0 to inform user no matches found.

issues.description: Element grid cells use `Clickable` wrapper with `tabIndex={0}` and `role='button'`, but keyboard interaction (Enter/Space to select) may not be wired.
issues.fix: Add `onKeyDown` handler to cells to detect Enter/Space and trigger selection for keyboard users.

issues.description: Legend section lacks semantic structure (e.g., no `<dl>` or `<fieldset>`). Color swatches have no explicit `aria-label` for color-blind users.
issues.fix: Wrap each legend item in a `<div>` with `role='group'` and add `aria-label` to color boxes (e.g., 'Category color: blue').

issues.description: Inline style object for grid is large and repeated; categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap duplicate logic (both define 'default' fallback differently).
issues.fix: Extract grid styles to a const; unify category color logic into single map with `{ category, bgColor, textColor }` structure.

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates solid fundamentals with proper use of design tokens, responsive search filtering, and good accessibility semantics (ARIA labels, semantic HTML). However, the rendered code is incomplete (detail panel cuts off mid-render), and interactive polish is missing — hover states are subtle, keyboard navigation may not work, and grid layout breaks when filtering. Code organization is clean overall, but could be refactored to reduce duplication in color mappings. With the truncation fixed and a few interactivity improvements (hover effects, keyboard handlers, empty state), this would be production-ready.

issues.description: The legend lists the categories but lacks the color swatches to identify which color belongs to which category.
issues.fix: Add a small colored circle or square next to each category name in the legend that matches the element card colors.

issues.description: The legend text is slightly cramped and lacks visual separation.
issues.fix: Increase spacing between legend items or wrap them in pill-shaped badges with corresponding background colors.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table layout is excellent, correctly using CSS grid to position elements according to their scientific groups and periods. The visual hierarchy within the element cards is clear and professional. However, the legend is practically unusable without color indicators, which was a specific requirement.

issues.description: Search filter logic is broken. filteredElements are computed but then rendered with opacity trick instead of actually filtering the grid. All 118 elements still render in the DOM, just hidden visually. This breaks the grid layout because hidden elements still occupy grid cells.
issues.fix: Change the grid rendering to only render elements that pass the filter: `{state.filteredElements.map((element) => (` and remove the opacity opacity ternary logic. Currently you're mapping over `state.filteredElements` but checking `!state.filteredElements.includes(element)` which is always false.

issues.description: Category legend includes all unique categories from props.elements, but doesn't filter them based on what's currently visible after search. When user searches for 'oxygen', the legend still shows all 10+ categories even though only nonmetal is displayed.
issues.fix: Update categories computation to use `filteredElements` instead of `props.elements`: `const cats = new Set(state.filteredElements.map(el => el.category).filter(Boolean));`

issues.description: Grid cells have onClick handler but keyboard navigation incomplete. aria-label is good, but no onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space to select element. Users must use mouse.
issues.fix: Add onKeyDown handler: `onKeyDown={(e) => (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') && state.handleElementClick(element)}`

issues.description: Element cells lack hover state visual feedback. At 200ms ease transitions are defined but no :hover styling provided. Buttons in detail panel use variant='ghost' which may have insufficient contrast against white card background.
issues.fix: Add hover state styling to element cells (scale or shadow change). Consider using variant='outline' for close button or explicitly set contrast.

issues.description: Detail panel appears with animation but no fade-in keyframes defined in component. CSS animation 'fadeIn' is referenced but never defined, so transition doesn't work.
issues.fix: Add CSS or use Framer Motion, or replace with opacity transition: `style={{ opacity: 0, animation: 'none' }}` and add mount transition, or use simpler approach without animation.

issues.description: categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap are hardcoded with 10 entries but data contract doesn't specify what categories exist. If elements have unexpected category values, they get fallback 'default' key that doesn't exist in maps, resulting in undefined CSS variables.
issues.fix: Add a safe getter function: `const getColor = (cat) => categoryColorMap[cat] || 'var(--ggui-color-outline, #d4d4d8)';` (already done for fallback, but consider making it cleaner)

issues.description: Detail panel close button has aria-label but card itself uses aria-label='Element details' which is redundant with Heading. Focus management not addressed—when panel closes, focus should return to the element that was clicked.
issues.fix: Implement focus trap in detail panel and restore focus to clicked element on close using useRef and useEffect.

issues.description: Code snippet is truncated. Category field in detail panel is incomplete (text cuts off mid-line). Assume it continues but this indicates incomplete implementation.
issues.fix: Complete the Category field rendering and ensure all detail fields are displayed.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table component has a solid structure with good use of design tokens, semantic HTML, and mostly clean code. However, it has a critical flaw: the search filter doesn't actually filter the grid rendering—it just hides elements with opacity, breaking the CSS Grid layout. Additionally, hover states are missing, keyboard navigation incomplete, and the fade-in animation is referenced but not defined. With these interactivity and completeness fixes, this would be a strong component.

issues.description: The category legend contains only text strings and lacks the actual color indicators (swatches) requested to help users identify categories on the map.
issues.fix: Apply the corresponding category background or border colors to the legend items, preferably as small colored squares or pills.

issues.description: No visible detail panel or placeholder for element details below the grid.
issues.fix: Add a placeholder state below the grid that says 'Select an element for more details' to indicate where information will appear.

issues.description: The Category Legend section is quite plain and lacks professional styling compared to the grid.
issues.fix: Wrap legend items in badges or use a grid/flex layout with proper spacing and vertical alignment for the color swatches.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table grid layout is exceptionally well-executed, accurately placing elements including the lanthanide and actinide series. However, the legend is visually incomplete as it provides the category names without their associated colors, and the missing initial state for the details panel makes the bottom of the page feel abrupt.

issues.description: Code is truncated mid-implementation. The detail panel closes abruptly after 'Atomic Weight' section, missing Category display and proper component closure.
issues.fix: Complete the detail panel with category display and close the Stack/Card/Container properly

issues.description: useComponent hook references undefined 'state.filteredElements' in categories useMemo. Should reference 'filteredElements' directly.
issues.fix: Change 'state.filteredElements.map' to 'filteredElements.map' in categories useMemo dependency

issues.description: useComponent hook returns object but state is used as 'state' variable naming without clear distinction. Logic is correct but naming could be clearer.
issues.fix: Consider renaming returned object or component structure for clarity

issues.description: Element cards lack visible hover state feedback. While transition is defined, no scale, shadow, or opacity change on hover is implemented.
issues.fix: Add ':hover' styles with scale(1.05) or shadow elevation to element cards using inline styles with :hover selector or Hoverable wrapper

issues.description: Search field doesn't show visual feedback (e.g., result count) when filtering. Users don't see how many elements match.
issues.fix: Add text below search field showing '{filteredElements.length} elements found' with design tokens for color

issues.description: Element cards use both background color AND 2px border, creating visual heaviness. Border color matches text, making it hard to distinguish.
issues.fix: Use subtle border with 1px width or remove border entirely, letting background color be primary visual indicator

issues.description: Legend wraps horizontally but may overflow on smaller screens. No responsive wrapping strategy.
issues.fix: Use responsive grid or flex-wrap in legend, or convert to vertical stack on mobile breakpoints

issues.description: Element cards use tabIndex={0} but no keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space to select). Focus management could be improved.
issues.fix: Add onKeyDown handler to element cards to handle Enter and Space keys for keyboard activation

issues.description: categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap are hardcoded. If new categories are added to data, colors won't auto-generate.
issues.fix: Consider generating color palette dynamically or adding fallback logic for unmapped categories

issues.description: Animation uses 'ggui-slideInUp' class name directly but no guarantee this exists in design system. Should verify or use CSS-in-JS.
issues.fix: Verify animation class exists in @ggui-ai/design or use inline keyframes/CSS

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates a solid understanding of the periodic table layout and implements most core features (grid positioning, search filtering, category legend, detail panel). However, the code is incomplete (truncated mid-implementation), contains a reference bug in the categories hook, and lacks polish in hover interactions and keyboard navigation. Visual hierarchy is good with clear separation of legend and grid, but element cards need refinement in their border/color treatment to reduce visual noise. With completion and the interactive polish additions, this would be production-ready.

issues.description: The category legend contains only text labels and lacks the corresponding color swatches, making it difficult to map colors to element types.
issues.fix: Add a small colored circle or square next to each category name in the legend that matches the element styling.

issues.description: The search input is very wide and lacks visual prominence or an icon to identify its purpose quickly.
issues.fix: Limit the max-width of the search bar, add a search icon, and perhaps a subtle shadow or border-color on focus.

issues.description: Text within some element cells (e.g., Praseodymium) is very cramped and close to the borders.
issues.fix: Slightly reduce font size for longer element names or increase the base cell size/spacing.

evaluations.critique: The component correctly implements the complex 18-column CSS grid required for a periodic table, which is a significant technical win. The visual styling is clean and professional, though the legend is currently ineffective as it provides no visual link to the element colors. The overall layout is solid and achieves the primary goal well.

issues.description: Code is truncated mid-render. The detail panel closing tag, category display, and component closing are missing. The detail panel implementation is incomplete.
issues.fix: Complete the component by adding closing tags for the detail panel Stack, Card, and main Container. Ensure all nested props are fully rendered.

issues.description: Reference to undefined 'state.filteredElements' in categories useMemo. Should be 'filteredElements' (the local variable).
issues.fix: Change 'state.filteredElements' to 'filteredElements' in the categories useMemo dependency and logic.

issues.description: Hardcoded category-to-color mapping with fallback CSS variables mixed in. Not scalable if new categories are added; should derive from data or use a more dynamic approach.
issues.fix: Consider generating categoryColorMap from element data or using a centralized, extensible configuration object separate from component logic.

issues.description: No focus/keyboard handling on element cells. aria-label and tabIndex={0} are set but no onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space to activate click.
issues.fix: Add onKeyDown handler to element boxes to trigger click on Enter/Space key press for full keyboard accessibility.

issues.description: Element cells have both background color AND border with category text color. Border may be redundant/visually cluttered. Consider using border as accent only.
issues.fix: Remove border or use a lighter border variant (0.5px, lower opacity) to reduce visual noise.

issues.description: Search filter removes elements from grid but provides no visual feedback or message when no results match. User may be confused.
issues.fix: Add conditional rendering: if filteredElements is empty, show an empty state message (e.g., 'No elements found').

issues.description: Legend category names are lowercase (e.g., 'noble-gas'). Should be title-cased or formatted for readability.
issues.fix: Transform category names with a utility function (e.g., 'noble-gas' → 'Noble Gas') when rendering in legend.

issues.description: Inline animation style 'fadeIn' is used but not defined anywhere. MotionKeyframes is imported but may not include this animation.
issues.fix: Define fadeIn keyframes or use a standard animation from the design system (e.g., 'ggui-fadeIn' if available).

issues.description: Detail panel closes with button but no trap focus or backdrop. Keyboard users could tab outside the panel.
issues.fix: Consider adding a Modal wrapper or focus trap to the detail panel for better UX.

issues.description: Missing default props for optional fields in element data (atomicWeight, category). Defensive checks are present but could be cleaner.
issues.fix: Add a helper function to normalize element data or use nullish coalescing more consistently.

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates good structure and accessibility fundamentals with semantic HTML and ARIA labels, but is **incomplete due to truncation** and has a critical logic error in the categories useMemo. The 18-column grid layout is properly implemented, search filtering works, and the color-coding system uses design tokens effectively. However, the code lacks polished interactivity (no keyboard support for element cells, no empty-state feedback), and the detail panel implementation is cut off. Fix the truncation, correct the state reference bug, and add keyboard event handling to bring this to production quality.

issues.description: The category legend lists the names of the categories but does not show the associated colors/swatches.
issues.fix: Add color swatches or color the text in the legend to match the element categories.

issues.description: The search input is unnecessarily wide, spanning the entire page width.
issues.fix: Restrict the search input to a maximum width (e.g., 400px) or center it.

issues.description: The element cells feel a bit 'empty' because only the border and text are colored; solid background fills would improve readability.
issues.fix: Apply a light background color (tint) to the element cells based on their category.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table layout is technically perfect, utilizing the 18-column grid exactly as requested. The hierarchy is clear, and the element symbols are prominent. However, the legend is non-functional as it lacks the color keys, and the search bar could benefit from better sizing.

issues.description: Detail panel is incomplete. The code cuts off mid-line and doesn't show atomicWeight field or full element details layout.
issues.fix: Complete the detail panel implementation showing atomicWeight and category fields with proper Stack layout and styling.

issues.description: Search filtering doesn't hide elements in grid—it filters the legend but all elements remain visible with gridRow/gridColumn placement. When searching, filtered elements should still occupy their grid positions (gaps appear) or grid should be rebuilt.
issues.fix: Either: (1) Keep all elements visible but dim non-matching ones, or (2) Use CSS visibility/display tricks to maintain grid structure while hiding non-matches, or (3) Only render filtered elements and accept grid gaps.

issues.description: Element cells at minHeight 80px may cause layout imbalance for a real periodic table. Some cells might feel cramped or stretched depending on content.
issues.fix: Adjust minHeight and padding to provide consistent visual balance across all element cells, or use aspect-ratio for uniform sizing.

issues.description: Legend uses hardcoded padding (6px) instead of design token spacing variable.
issues.fix: Replace padding={6} with var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px) or equivalent design token.

issues.description: Element cells have transition on 'all' but no hover state (scale, shadow, or background change) defined. Hover feedback is weak.
issues.fix: Add :hover pseudo-class or use Hoverable wrapper to darken/scale element on hover for better visual feedback.

issues.description: Detail panel fadeIn animation is custom CSS string, not using MotionKeyframes pattern like grid cells do. Inconsistent animation approach.
issues.fix: Use MotionKeyframes component consistently for the detail panel animation, or define keyframes in a stylesheet.

issues.description: Element cells have role='button' but are Box elements. Should use Button component or ensure Clickable component properly exposes button semantics.
issues.fix: Verify Clickable exposes proper button ARIA semantics; consider using <button> semantics explicitly if Clickable doesn't.

issues.description: Search input uses SearchField but no aria-label or descriptive label visible. Accessibility could be clearer.
issues.fix: Add aria-label='Search elements by name or symbol' to SearchField for clarity.

issues.description: Code is truncated—last closing elements for detail panel are cut off. File appears incomplete, making it impossible to verify full component renders correctly.
issues.fix: Provide complete source code including all closing tags and final export statement.

issues.description: categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap have hardcoded category keys. Real periodic table likely needs more categories or a dynamic mapping system.
issues.fix: Consider deriving colors dynamically from category names or accepting a categoryColorMap prop.

evaluations.critique: The component has solid structure with good use of design tokens, proper state management, and keyboard navigation support. However, the code is incomplete (truncated mid-detail-panel), search filtering doesn't properly hide filtered elements from the grid, and hover states on element cells are missing. Completing the detail panel implementation and adding visual feedback on element interactions would bring this to production-ready quality.

issues.description: The category legend contains text labels but lacks the actual color indicators (swatches) to identify them on the map.
issues.fix: Add color swatches (circles or squares) next to each label in the legend that match the category's background color.

issues.description: The legend section is just a plain horizontal list of text, which looks unstyled compared to the element grid.
issues.fix: Apply better spacing, consistent casing, and background chips to the legend items.

issues.description: The search input is full-width, which looks slightly unbalanced relative to the table's width.
issues.fix: Apply a max-width to the search container or group it with other controls.

evaluations.critique: The component excels at the complex task of rendering the 18-column periodic table grid accurately, including the correct placement of Lanthanides and Actinides. However, the legend is functionally incomplete as it lacks color swatches, making the color-coding difficult to interpret without guessing. The technical structure is solid, but the UI needs a more polished legend and a clearer 'empty state' for the detail panel.

issues.description: Detail panel is incomplete — code cuts off mid-way. The atomicWeight field is never rendered, and the detail panel layout is unfinished. The close button uses Icon component that may not exist in the design system.
issues.fix: Complete the detail panel Stack with all fields (symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category) and replace Icon with a properly available component or use text-based close button.

issues.description: Legend only shows categories present in filtered elements. If all elements are filtered out, legend becomes empty and less useful for discovering all possible categories.
issues.fix: Extract unique categories from ALL props.elements (not just filteredElements) for the legend, so users always see the complete category legend.

issues.description: Element cells use both background AND border color (from categoryColorMap/TextColorMap). This creates visual redundancy. Border color matches text color, making borders hard to see on light backgrounds.
issues.fix: Use border-color with reduced opacity (e.g., `rgba(..., 0.3)`) or remove border entirely and rely on background color alone for category distinction.

issues.description: Detail panel animation uses inline `animation: 'fadeIn 200ms ease-out'` but fadeIn keyframe is not defined. Animation will silently fail.
issues.fix: Either define the fadeIn keyframe in MotionKeyframes or use a CSS-in-JS solution (styled-components or emotion) with proper keyframe definition.

issues.description: Element cells have no visible hover state. Users get no visual feedback when hovering over an element before clicking, reducing discoverability.
issues.fix: Add `:hover` styles (transform, box-shadow, opacity change) to the element cell Box style with transition. Example: `transform: 'scale(1.05)'` on hover.

issues.description: Focus state on element cells relies on browser default. No custom focus indicator visible, reducing accessibility for keyboard users.
issues.fix: Add `:focus-visible` styling with outline or box-shadow (e.g., `outline: '2px solid var(--ggui-color-primary)'`).

issues.description: Element cells use `role='button'` and `tabIndex={0}` on Box elements. Semantically, these should be actual `<button>` elements or use a proper interactive wrapper.
issues.fix: Replace Box + Clickable with actual `<button>` elements or wrap in a semantically appropriate interactive component from the design system.

issues.description: SearchField uses placeholder text but no explicit label. Screen readers won't announce the purpose of the search input clearly.
issues.fix: Add an `aria-label='Search elements by name or symbol'` or wrap SearchField in a FormField with a visible label.

issues.description: Detail panel close button uses Icon component which may not have proper ARIA labels if the icon name is not recognized.
issues.fix: Ensure the Icon component supports aria-label, or use explicit text-based close button with proper contrast.

issues.description: Component renders keyframes as JSX (`{keyframes}`) inside the grid container. This is unconventional and may cause layout issues if MotionKeyframes renders DOM elements.
issues.fix: Move `{keyframes}` outside the grid or ensure MotionKeyframes is a style-only component that doesn't render DOM nodes.

issues.description: categoryColorMap and categoryTextColorMap are hard-coded for specific categories. If props.elements contains an unknown category, it defaults silently with fallback color.
issues.fix: Generate color mappings dynamically from a predefined palette, or document all supported categories clearly in comments.

evaluations.critique: The periodic table component has strong structure and good interactivity fundamentals (keyboard navigation, search filtering, detail panel), but suffers from incomplete code (detail panel cuts off mid-render), missing hover/focus states on interactive elements, and unresolved animations. The layout correctly uses CSS Grid with 18 columns and design tokens, but visual feedback for user interaction is minimal. Accessibility is decent with ARIA labels and keyboard support, though semantic HTML could be improved. Code quality is clean but has minor issues with JSX keyframe rendering and hard-coded category mappings.

issues.description: The legend lists category names as plain text without the required color indicators, making it non-functional as a legend.
issues.fix: Add colored boxes or text-background colors to the legend items that match the colors used in the periodic table grid.

issues.description: The detail panel is not visible in the screenshot, and there is no placeholder or empty state to indicate where element details will appear.

issues.description: The legend styling is very minimal (just a row of text), which lacks the professional finish of the table itself.

evaluations.critique: The 18-column grid layout is perfectly executed, correctly placing Lanthanides and Actinides in the standard IUPAC format which is often difficult for AI. issues.description: Detail panel is cut off mid-render. The component code ends abruptly at the detail panel section, missing the display of atomicWeight and complete element details. The detail panel structure is incomplete.

issues.description: CSS pseudo-states (:hover, :focus-visible) are defined in inline style object but won't work in JSX. React doesn't support pseudo-classes in inline styles. Hover/focus states won't be applied.
issues.fix: Create a separate CSS module or use styled component library to properly handle :hover and :focus-visible states, or use onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave for hover effects.

issues.description: Animation keyframe 'ggui-slideInUp' is referenced but may not be defined. Animation fallback exists but name should be verified against design system.

issues.description: Detail panel has 'fadeIn' animation in inline style but CSS animations don't work in inline styles without a stylesheet. Animation won't execute.

issues.description: Search filter works on full elements list but filtered elements still maintain grid positioning based on original row/col values. Elements disappear from grid when filtered rather than reflowing. This may be intentional but creates visual gaps.

issues.description: Detail panel close button uses text '✕' instead of Icon component. Inconsistent with design system patterns.

Once the detail panel is completed and animations/hover states are properly implemented via CSS modules or the design system's motion API, this will be a polished, production-ready component.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

issues.description: Image placeholder uses inline styles with hardcoded token fallback values instead of pure design tokens. The fallback colors are hardcoded hex values which violates the token-only requirement.
issues.fix: Replace inline backgroundColor and color with CSS class or wrap in Pressable/Hoverable to apply theme tokens consistently without fallbacks.

issues.description: Star rating icons don't have semantic meaning — both filled and empty stars use the same icon name 'star' with only color differentiation. Assistive technology cannot distinguish them without context.
issues.fix: Use separate icon names (e.g., 'star-filled' vs 'star-outline') or add aria-label to each star group like 'Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars'.

issues.description: Quantity input field is directly editable but validation happens only on blur/change. Typing invalid values (>stock or <1) has no immediate visual feedback.
issues.fix: Add real-time input validation with inline error state or use a controlled number input that prevents invalid entries before onChange fires.

issues.description: On mobile, the Stack with direction='horizontal' and wrap will stack the image and details vertically, but there's no explicit responsive container adjustment (no gap reduction on mobile for better UX).
issues.fix: Add responsive gap adjustment using media queries or a responsive utility, e.g., gap={[16, 32]} for mobile/desktop, or ensure padding scales on sm widths.

issues.description: Product reviewCount prop is defined in the contract but never used or displayed (e.g., 'Showing 3 reviews' or '4.5 ★ (128 reviews'). Partial feature implementation.
issues.fix: Display reviewCount near the rating stars, e.g., 'Rating: 4.5 ★ (128 reviews)' to fully leverage the contract.

The code is clean and maintainable with proper state management and event handling. Minor issues include hardcoded color fallbacks in inline styles (should use pure tokens), star rating icons lacking semantic distinction for accessibility, and the unused reviewCount prop—all easily fixable without architectural changes. Visual hierarchy is clear and responsive stacking is functional, though mobile spacing could be tightened for better aesthetics.

issues.fix: Make the stock status badge an inline-block element with padding or a pill shape instead of full-width.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

issues.description: Sender name is not displayed above message groups. The requirement states 'Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp' but only text and timestamp are visible.
issues.fix: Add a Text element displaying group.sender above each message group with a subtle color/size distinction.

issues.description: Message groups lack visual separation from sender names. The grouping logic works but there's no clear label showing who sent the group.
issues.fix: Display sender name as a small heading or label at the start of each group, styled with onSurfaceVariant color.

issues.description: The 70% maxWidth on messages can feel cramped on very wide screens and may not adapt well to different viewport sizes.
issues.fix: Consider using max-width: min(70%, 500px) or adjusting based on actual container width for better responsiveness.

issues.description: Input field lacks visible focus indicator styling and hover state definition. While functional, the UX could be more polished.
issues.fix: Add focus-visible and hover states to Input with outline or shadow using design tokens.

issues.description: No loading/sending indicator when onSendMessage is called. UX is unclear about whether message was sent.
issues.fix: Show a spinner or disable input during message send, or add an optimistic UI update with pending state.

issues.description: Sender names lack aria-labels or semantic markup distinguishing them from message content.
issues.fix: Wrap sender names in <strong> or add aria-label='Sent by [name]' for clarity.

issues.description: Message timestamps use <time> element correctly but no aria-label explaining the formatted time is abbreviated.
issues.fix: Add aria-label with full timestamp (e.g., aria-label='Sent at 2:30 PM') to clarify abbreviated display.

issues.description: Magic number '70%' for maxWidth appears without explanation; should be a design token or constant.
issues.fix: Extract to a named constant like MAX_MESSAGE_WIDTH = '70%' or define in design system.

These are refinements that would elevate it from good to production-ready.

issues.fix: Add the sender's name above or inside each message bubble, following the grouping logic requested.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Avatar selection buttons lack ARIA labels. Screen readers cannot identify which avatar is selected or what each option represents.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to each avatar Box (e.g., aria-label={`Select ${emoji} avatar`}) and aria-pressed={state.formData.avatar === emoji} for toggle semantics.

issues.description: FormField components wrapping Input have duplicate label attributes (label on both FormField and Input), causing unclear labeling for screen readers.
issues.fix: Remove label prop from Input components; let FormField handle labeling via aria-labelledby or htmlFor attribute.

issues.description: Avatar selection uses inline style border logic instead of Card or Badge primitive for consistency. Could use visual polish.
issues.fix: Consider wrapping avatars in a Badge component or use a consistent button-like style with hover effects for better visual hierarchy.

issues.description: Avatar selection buttons have transition but no visible hover state (cursor, opacity, or shadow change) for better UX feedback.
issues.fix: Add Hoverable wrapper or CSS hover styles (opacity/shadow) to avatar selection buttons: `:hover { opacity: 0.8; box-shadow: ... }`.

issues.description: Review summary section (step 3) lacks semantic structure. Field labels and values are in generic divs instead of definition lists.
issues.fix: Use <dl>, <dt>, <dd> elements or aria-labelledby to associate labels with values for better semantics.

issues.description: validateStep is called in both handleNext and isNextDisabled calculation, causing re-validation on every render. Inefficient for performance.
issues.fix: Memoize validation results with useMemo or only validate on Next click, not on every render cycle.

evaluations.critique: This is a well-structured, feature-complete onboarding wizard with smooth transitions, proper form validation, and clean state management. The layout is professional with good use of design tokens and spacing. However, accessibility needs improvement—avatar selection lacks ARIA labels/semantics, and duplicate form labels could confuse screen readers. Minor visual polish (hover states on avatars) and performance optimization (memoize validation) would elevate this to production-ready.

issues.description: Avatar selection Box uses Clickable wrapper but lacks role='radio' or role='button' semantics. Screen reader users won't know it's selectable. Also no aria-label per emoji.
issues.fix: Replace Box/Clickable with a proper RadioGroup or add role='radio' + aria-checked + aria-label='Select {emoji} avatar' to each avatar button.

issues.description: Step indicator text 'Step X of 3' is semantic but the progress bar div has no aria-label or role. Not clear to assistive tech what it represents.
issues.fix: Add role='progressbar' aria-valuenow={currentStep} aria-valuemin={1} aria-valuemax={3} to the progress bar container.

issues.description: FormField components may not have proper label associations if Input/Select don't auto-connect. No explicit htmlFor on labels.
issues.fix: Ensure FormField properly associates labels via htmlFor/id, or verify @ggui-ai/design FormField handles this internally.

issues.description: validateEmail function defined in hook but also used inline in useMemo. Closure capture works but slightly redundant.
issues.fix: Move validateEmail outside useComponent or memoize it to avoid recreating on every render.

issues.description: formData state updates don't trigger re-validation on step 2; isNextDisabled only checks step 1. Step 2 has no next button validation.
issues.fix: Either add step 2 validation logic or clarify that step 2 is always available to proceed (currently works but is implicit).

evaluations.critique: The wizard is functionally complete with smooth transitions, proper step progression, and email validation. Visual design is clean with good use of design tokens and spacing. However, accessibility needs improvement—avatar selection lacks ARIA semantics (should be RadioGroup), the progress bar needs aria-label, and focus states are missing. Interactivity is polished but lacks submit loading state and explicit hover feedback on avatar buttons. Code is well-structured but has minor opportunities to reduce redundancy and clarify validation intent across steps.
